title: ETS table registry and ownership model
purpose: ETS (Erlang Term Storage) provides shared mutable tables accessible by multiple processes. Every other ETS brief builds on this one — the table registry, table IDs, ownership model, and access protection levels are the foundation. A table is owned by a process; when the owner dies, the table is deleted (unless an heir is configured — B-099). Access protection determines which processes can read/write.
task: Define the ETS table registry in SharedState, the `EtsTable` trait, table metadata (name, ID, type, protection, owner), and the table ID allocation scheme.

requirements.title: EtsTableId and table registry in SharedState
requirements.spec: Add a table registry to SharedState that maps table IDs to table instances.
acceptance: `EtsTableId` is a unique u64, monotonically increasing (never reused)
`SharedState` gains `ets_tables: DashMap<EtsTableId, Arc<dyn EtsTable>>`
`SharedState::create_table(metadata) -> EtsTableId` allocates an ID and inserts the table
`SharedState::lookup_table(id) -> Option<Arc<dyn EtsTable>>` retrieves a table
`SharedState::delete_table(id) -> bool` removes a table from the registry
Named tables can also be looked up by atom: `SharedState::lookup_table_by_name(name: Atom) -> Option<EtsTableId>`
Test: create table, look up by ID, delete, verify gone

requirements.title: EtsTable trait and table metadata
requirements.spec: Define the trait that all table types implement, plus the metadata struct.
acceptance: `EtsTableMetadata` struct: `name: Option<Atom>`, `id: EtsTableId`, `table_type: EtsTableType`, `protection: Protection`, `owner: u64` (PID), `keypos: usize` (1-based element position for key, default 1)
`EtsTableType` enum: `Set`, `OrderedSet`, `Bag`, `DuplicateBag`
`Protection` enum: `Public` (any process R/W), `Protected` (owner W, any R), `Private` (owner only)

`EtsTable` trait with methods: `metadata(&self) -> &EtsTableMetadata`, `insert(&self, tuple: Term) -> Result<(), EtsError>`, `lookup(&self, key: Term) -> Vec<Term>`, `delete_key(&self, key: Term) -> bool`, `tab2list(&self) -> Vec<Term>`
Test: create metadata, verify all fields accessible

requirements.title: Access control enforcement
requirements.spec: Implement protection level checks that gate read/write access based on the calling process's PID and the table's protection level.
acceptance: `EtsTable::check_access(caller_pid: u64, operation: AccessOp) -> Result<(), EtsError>` where AccessOp is Read or Write
Public: any PID can read or write
Protected: any PID can read, only owner can write
Private: only owner can read or write
`EtsError::AccessDenied { table_id, caller_pid, operation }` for violations
Test: private table rejects non-owner read and write
Test: protected table allows non-owner read but rejects non-owner write
Test: public table allows everything

requirements.title: Owner death cleanup
requirements.spec: When a process dies, all ETS tables it owns are deleted from the registry (unless heir is configured — B-099 adds that; for now, always delete).
acceptance: Process cleanup path (supervision exit signal propagation) checks for owned tables
All tables owned by the dying process are removed from the registry
Table data is dropped (Arc ref count goes to 0 if no other references)
Test: create table, kill owner process, verify table is deleted

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement any table type (set, bag, ordered_set) — those are B-091 through B-093
SHALL NOT implement any ETS BIFs — those are B-094+
SHALL NOT implement heir or give_away — that's B-099
SHALL implement the registry, ownership, protection model, and table lifecycle only
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
